S.text: Comprehensive exam in a patient with type 2 diabetes ~9 years, per PCP referral for annual diabetic eye exam.
Reported glycemic control: last A1C ~7.8; on metformin.
Intermittent transient blur associated with elevated blood sugar; denies floaters, flashes, dark spots.

O.text: Pupils equal and reactive, no afferent pupillary defect; confrontation fields full OU.
Manifest refraction OD +1.25 -0.50 x 090, VA cc 20/25; OS +1.00 -0.75 x 085, VA cc 20/25; mild hyperopia with astigmatism; +2.00 near add.
Slit lamp: lids and conjunctiva quiet, corneas clear, anterior chambers deep; mild nuclear sclerotic cataract OU, not visually significant.
Dilated fundus: optic nerves healthy C/D 0.4 OU; scattered dot-and-blot hemorrhages and microaneurysms OU; hard exudates temporal to macula OD.
Maculae clinically uninvolved without visible edema; central vision preserved OU.

A.text: Mild nuclear sclerotic cataract OU, visually insignificant.
Hyperopia with astigmatism and presbyopia OU.

P.text: Order OCT of both maculae today to rule out subclinical diabetic macular edema.
Shorten follow-up to 6 months to monitor retinopathy (rather than annual).
Send report to PCP emphasizing tighter glycemic control; patient to call sooner for new floaters, blurring, or dark spots.
Update spectacle Rx with new refraction.
